title: Change Manifest
description: One file per unit of work at specs/changes/<slug>.change.json. A change is the primary unit of work; areas/contracts remain the logical spec boundary. The manifest holds membership only — which targets, which IDs. Per-target phase status (checked/applied/verified) is NEVER stored here: it is derived mechanically from the area JSONs (witness model_sha freshness, check_results.ran_at vs last_modified, traceability, verification_log) so it cannot go stale. Committed with the PR; status 'landed' on merge makes it a changelog entry. Deleting every manifest leaves the specs complete.

change.description: Slug. Doubles as the suggested branch name (change/<slug>) and commit scope (spec(<slug>): ...).

intent.description: One sentence: what this change accomplishes, in domain terms.

status.description: open = created, no spec edits yet; in-progress = edits underway; landed = PR merged; abandoned = dropped without landing.

branch.description: Git branch carrying the change, if one was created.

targets.description: Every area/contract spec this change touches. Contracts spanning a touched area are auto-added (auto: true). Membership and IDs only — phase status is derived from the area JSONs, never stored.

auto.description: true when pulled in automatically (contract cascade) rather than edited explicitly.

ids.description: REQ/INV/PROP/CON/DEC/Q IDs added or modified in this target by this change. References only — content lives in the area JSON.

open_questions.description: Cross-target question refs in <area>/Q-NNN form, blocking this change.
